zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Somery於大約1233年出生在英國史洛普爾艾勒斯莫時，她的父親Sir Roger IV de Somery Baron of Dudley40歲，母親Nichole d'Aubigny24歲。她於大約1253年與John le Strange IV在英國英格蘭史洛普爾艾勒斯莫結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1282年在英國史洛普爾勒頓 希 堂斯享年50歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de Somery于大约1233年出生在英国史洛普爾艾勒斯莫时，她的父亲Sir Roger IV de Somery Baron of Dudley40岁，母亲Nichole d'Aubigny24岁。她于大约1253年与John le Strange IV在英国英格兰史洛普爾艾勒斯莫结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1282年在英国史洛普爾勒頓 希 堂斯享年50岁。

nameMeanings.name: Somery
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Abelino, Adela, Alberto, Alfredo, Ana, Angel, Asuncion, Domingo, Elena, Emiliano, Emilio, Eusebio.
Spanish: possibly a habitational name from La Somera in Cuenca province, named from somero ‘shallow’. This surname is most common in the Philippines.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Somery was born about 1233, in Ellesmere, Shropshire, England, her father, Sir Roger IV de Somery Baron of Dudley, was 40 and her mother, Nichole d'Aubigny, was 24. She married John le Strange IV about 1253, in Ellesmere, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died in 1282, in Ruyton XI Towns, Shropshire, England, at the age of 50.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan de Somery est née environ 1233, à Ellesmere, Shropshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Roger IV de Somery Baron of Dudley, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Nichole d'Aubigny, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé John le Strange IV environ 1253, à Ellesmere, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1282, à Ruyton XI Towns, Shropshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 50 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan de Somery um 1233 in Ellesmere, Shropshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Roger IV de Somery Baron of Dudley 40 und ihre Mutter Nichole d'Aubigny 24. Sie hat um 1253 in Ellesmere, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom John le Strange IV geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1282 im Alter von 50 Jahren in Ruyton XI Towns, Shropshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Somery è nata il circa 1233 nel Ellesmere, Shropshire, England, sua padre, Sir Roger IV de Somery Baron of Dudley, aveva 40 anni e sua madre, Nichole d'Aubigny, aveva 24 anni. Lei sposato John le Strange IV circa 1253, nel Ellesmere, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1282, nel Ruyton XI Towns, Shropshire, England, all'età di 50 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan de Someryが約1233年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアエレスメアで生まれた時，父Sir Roger IV de Somery Baron of Dudleyは40歳，母Nichole d'Aubignyは24歳であった。約1253年にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアエレスメアでJohn le Strange IVと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1282年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアルイトン クシ トウンスで50歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Somery(이)가 대략 1233년 영국 슈럽실르 엘레스머에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Roger IV de Somery Baron of Dudley(은)는 40세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Nichole d'Aubigny(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 대략 1253년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 엘레스머에서 John le Strange IV(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1282년 영국 슈럽실르 루이턴 스이 타운스에서 50세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Somery nasceu aproximadamente 1233, em Ellesmere, Shropshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Roger IV de Somery Baron of Dudley, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Nichole d'Aubigny, tinha 24 anos. Ela casou-se com John le Strange IV aproximadamente 1253, em Ellesmere, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1282, em Ruyton XI Towns, Shropshire, Inglaterra, com 50 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de Somery родилась около 1233 г. в местности эллесмир, шропшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Roger IV de Somery Baron of Dudley было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Nichole d'Aubigny, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John le Strange IV около 1253 г. в местности эллесмир, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1282 г. в местности руитон кси тоунс, шропшир, Англия в возрасте 50 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan de Somery nació aproximadamente 1233, en Ellesmere, Shropshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Roger IV de Somery Baron of Dudley, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Nichole d'Aubigny, tenía 24 años. Se casó con John le Strange IV aproximadamente 1253, en Ellesmere, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió en 1282, en Ruyton XI Towns, Shropshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 50 años.
